title: chroot: allow to run with a single mapping
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind bug

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

allow to use chroot with a single user available.

#### How to verify it

follow the reproducer here: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/2459

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

Fixes 2459

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes please follow the kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
NONE
```

comments.body: @giuseppe @rhatdan will this fix enable chroot isolation for podman build without need of crun/runc? I am considering moving to podman since it  provides python SDK. Thanks

> @giuseppe @rhatdan will this fix enable chroot isolation for podman build without need of crun/runc? I am considering moving to podman since it provides python SDK. Thanks

yes, you can use `--isolation=chroot` and have only one ID available
